description_long: This is a large, amphora-style vase with a wide mouth, two robust handles, and a rounded body tapering to a narrow foot. The surface is adorned with intricate black-figure artwork, depicting scenes from ancient Greek mythology, including warriors in combat and figures engaged in ritual or social activities. The rim and base are finished in a matte black, contrasting with the ochre and terracotta hues of the main body. Decorative patterns, such as geometric borders and swirling motifs, encircle the neck and rear of the vase. The handles are thick and arching, providing both functional grip and visual balance. The overall craftsmanship and detailed ornamentation suggest it is a display piece, likely intended for ceremonial or decorative use.
description: A large, amphora-style vase with black-figure Greek artwork, ornate patterns, and two sturdy handles. The body features mythological scenes and geometric motifs in ochre and black.
description_view_0: Front view showing a dramatic black-figure combat scene with two warriors, detailed border at the neck, and both handles visible.
description_view_1: Rear angled view highlighting the swirling ochre patterns and one handle, with no figural artwork visible.
description_view_2: Opposite front view displaying a different black-figure scene with two standing figures, bordered by decorative motifs and both handles visible.
description_view_3: Rear angled view similar to view 1, showing swirling patterns and a single handle, with no figural decoration.
